title: Mass. family, Worcester woman identified as victims in deadly Virginia bus crash
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four members of a Greenfield family and a Worcester woman died in a Virginia bus crash on Interstate 81.
Victims Dmitri, Ecaterina, Emily, and Mark Doncev, and Priscilla Mafalda, were identified by Virginia State Police.
Bus driver Jing S. Dong charged with involuntary manslaughter after failing to slow and causing the crash.

title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong contributed three RBIs in the Red Sox's recent win, potentially improving his status in the lineup.
Despite diminished playing time due to other catchers, Wong hit his first non-homer RBI double since 2024.
Wong's current .245 batting average is better than Carlos Narvaez's, but lags behind Mickey Gasper's .340 average.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings, striking out seven, in the Red Sox's 9-1 win over the Guardians.
Gray maintained a 2.00 ERA over five starts in May, solidifying his role in Boston's rotation.
The Red Sox are 7-3 in Gray's starts this season, with Gray earning six wins and lowering his ERA to 3.06.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game at Polar Park in Worcester features athletes and celebrities from Boston sports.
Participants include Julian Edelman, Zdeno Chara, comedians Matt Rife and Dave Portnoy, and coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels.
The event benefits the MayeDay foundation, supporting children and families facing hardship, with doors opening at 5 p.m. Sunday.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots are reportedly poised to acquire A.J. Brown from the Eagles with a trade framework in place.
A.J. Brown trade expected to complete post-June 1 to minimize Eagles' salary cap hit.
Patriots hesitant to trade a first-round pick, complicating negotiations per NFL Network's Ian Rapoport.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A loud explosion around 2:10 PM on Saturday was reported across Greater Boston, causing widespread concern.
Meteorologists like Nick Stewart suggested the boom was from a bolide meteor, detected east of Boston by satellites.
No earthquakes or lightning were detected, and officials reported no emergency incidents related to the boom.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, contributing to the Red Sox's 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts in a new bulk role.
The Red Sox are struggling at 23-33, having lost six of their last seven games, and sit in last place in the AL East.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Boston Celtics fans and the TD Garden environment during a recent livestream amid trade rumors.
The Miami Heat and Boston Celtics are leading contenders to acquire Antetokounmpo, who wishes to stay in the Eastern Conference.
Questions persist about Antetokounmpo's fit with Celtics' Jayson Tatum, given spacing concerns and Antetokounmpo's shooting ability.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared Harvard's Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd and safety concerns.
Massachusetts State Police responded at 5 a.m. to reports of blocked pathways and attempted river swimming.
The event coincided with Harvard's 2026 class commencement, featuring Conan O'Brien as keynote speaker.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now streaming on Peacock, featuring Uma Thurman in a combined version of Tarantino's films.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2, starring Tina Fey and Steve Carell, is available for streaming on Netflix.
'Spider-Noir', starring Nicolas Cage as a 1930s Spider-Man, can be streamed on Prime Video.
